answer:
**Allama Iqbal's poem "Hindustani Bachon Ka Qaumi Geet" (National Song of the Children of Hindustan), from *Bang-E-Dara Part 1* (1924), is popularly known as "Sare Jahan Se Achha Hindustan Hamara" ("Better than the entire world is Hindustan, our homeland").**[^1]  
It celebrates India (Hindostan) as the homeland, invoking its spiritual, historical, and cultural significance.[^2]

Here is the English translation:

> Where Chishti first Truth's holy call conveyed,[^3]  
> And Nanak's song of Oneness was displayed,  
> The land the Tatars for their homeland made,  
> Which drew Hijaz's sons from desert shade,  
> This is my homeland, this land of mine.  
> 
> The land that left the wisest Greeks spellbound,[^4]  
> Whose arts and knowledge spread the world around,  
> Whose very dust by Truth with gold was crowned,  
> Where for the Turks great diamond wealth was found,  
> This is my homeland, this land of mine.  
> 
> The stars that fell from Persia's shattered space,[^5]  
> Were given light and a new shining place,  
> The world first heard of Oneness from this place,  
> A cooling breeze reached Arab's Chief with grace,  
> This is my homeland, this land of mine.  
> 
> Its people Moses, and its peaks are Sinai's height,[^6]  
> Where Prophet Noah's ark first saw the light,  
> Whose very ground is a stair to heaven's light,  
> To live here is a paradise of pure delight,  
> This is my homeland, this land of mine.[^7]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 26: "Hindustani Bachon Ka Qaumi Geet" ([chapter](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/26-hindustani-bachon-ka-qaumi-geet/translation/)).

[^2]: Ibid., wiki card: distinctive trigrams include "homeland land mine"; key passage opens "Where Chishti first Truth's holy call conveyed... This is my homeland, this land of mine."

[^3]: Ibid., [p-da6786](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/26-hindustani-bachon-ka-qaumi-geet/translation/#p-da6786).

[^4]: Ibid., [p-850e5c](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/26-hindustani-bachon-ka-qaumi-geet/translation/#p-850e5c).

[^5]: Ibid., [p-5d32a9](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/26-hindustani-bachon-ka-qaumi-geet/translation/#p-5d32a9).

[^6]: Ibid., [p-a9f72d](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/26-hindustani-bachon-ka-qaumi-geet/translation/#p-a9f72d).

[^7]: Full chapter: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 26 ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/26-hindustani-bachon-ka-qaumi-geet/translation/]).
